primary_argument: NVDA is capturing maximum momentum at its 10-day high of $204.12, riding a +4.20% daily surge that validates its role as the undisputed demand anchor for the accelerating Blackwell GPU allocation cycle and sovereign AI infrastructure build-out.
supporting_evidence: Price action just broke through short-term resistance to close exactly at its 10-day high of $204.12 on a +4.20% daily gain, confirming immediate institutional accumulation; upstream chain validation is undeniable as NVDA's move leads parallel strength in AVGO (+5.66%) and SMH (+2.97%), proving hyperscaler CapEx flows are actively pulling the entire semiconductor complex higher; macro tailwinds are optimal for thematic growth with the Fed paused, 10Y UST stable at 4.2%, and VIX at 16.9, eliminating rate-driven volatility drag and allowing capital to rotate aggressively into AI infrastructure leaders.
key_assumption: Hyperscaler CapEx commitments will continue to accelerate through the Blackwell allocation cycle while export controls remain static, ensuring NVDA's GPU supply chain maintains unbroken pricing power and volume expansion.
regime_sensitivity: In a low_vix environment, NVDA's upside ceiling expands rapidly as risk-on capital chases thematic momentum; in a high_vix scenario, the structural bull case remains intact because sovereign AI mandates and long-term hyperscaler contracts provide demand floor resilience, though accumulation pace would require scaling into dips rather than chasing breakouts.
watch_items: Hyperscaler FY2026 CapEx guidance is revised downward by more than 15% quarter-over-quarter
US export control policy restricts Blackwell GPU shipments to fewer than 40% of projected global data center capacity
